description: Save a hard-won lesson or surprising fix so future sessions benefit. Use when: "save this to knowledge", "record this", "remember for next time", "/learn", "記下來", "存到 knowledge", "記住這個", user shares a gotcha or non-obvious fix, solution took 2+ attempts, environment-specific workaround discovered. Also handles: "knowledge health audit", "check MEMORY.md size", stale knowledge cleanup. Not for: active debugging, writing tests, or project status updates.

results.query: I just spent 2 hours figuring out that sqlx::query! macros don't work with CTEs that have RETURNING clauses in offline mode. Save this to knowledge so we don't waste time again.
/learn build cargo watch ignores .sqlx/ changes — need to add --watch .sqlx to the command
record this: when running migrations in Docker, the DATABASE_URL needs to use the container hostname (db) not localhost. Took me 3 tries to figure out.
can you do a knowledge health audit? check if MEMORY.md is getting too long and whether any knowledge files are stale

the pg_try_advisory_lock vs pg_try_advisory_xact_lock distinction almost bit us again. Make sure this is recorded in knowledge — session-scoped locks don't release when connections return to the pool.
write a unit test for the advisory lock helper function. Cover the happy path and the timeout case.
debug why the migration is failing — it says 'relation already exists' but I dropped it in the previous migration
give me a retrospective on last week's commits — velocity, test ratio, focus areas
read .claude/knowledge/build-errors.md and tell me what we know about sqlx offline mode issues
update the project README with the current status of the billing engine — phase 2 is done, phase 3 is in progress
